description:
Jira: TBD
(Jira MCP unavailable at /distill time — record TBD; PR-creation hook should backfill.)

No spec subject (epic groups stages; each stage carries its own Advances:)

Distilled from /mini-plan run 20260517-200928-claude-mini.
Plan source: ~/Documents/my-vault/LLMs/plans/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/2026-05-17-archive-install-fix/runs/20260517-200928-claude-mini/05-refined-plan.md
Plan epic: think-ijk (bw-plan, closed)
Implementation epic mirror: think-pvr (bw-plan, will close at end of /distill)

Goal: repair the mix mutagen runtime preamble so that mix archive.install ./mutagen_ex-X.Y.Z.ez followed by mix mutagen works in a host project that does NOT declare :mutagen_ex in its mix.exs. Adds mutagen.cli.r16, scenarios s14c/s14d, verification v11 to .spec/specs/cli.spec.md. The path: / hex / git dep adoption paths covered by r14 are byte-for-byte unaffected — the repair branch is gated by the failure-tuple shape from Application.ensure_all_started/1 and never runs on the success path.

Branch already created: think-pvr/archive-install-fix (worktree: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-archive-install-fix).

comments.text: Orchestrator scope expansion: spawned mutagen-wrd.40.4 to fix a :code.add_pathz/1 charlist/binary bug in S1's runtime preamble repair that S2's end-to-end integration test exposed (the test S1 explicitly deferred to S2). S2 (mutagen-wrd.40.2) is now blocked by mutagen-wrd.40.4. S1 (mutagen-wrd.40.1) was unit-test green but the s14c success-after-self-heal scenario could only be validated end-to-end in S2 — and it is what surfaced the bug. S3 (mutagen-wrd.40.3) already closed independently and is merged into the epic at 677a209.

Orchestrator paused — second product-level gap discovered in S2's integration test after S1b's charlist fix landed. mutagen_ex's transitive dep `telemetry` is not bundled in `mix archive.build`'s output, so an archive-installed `mix mutagen` against a host that doesn't declare telemetry can't start. See mutagen-wrd.40.2 most recent comment (ORCHESTRATOR_ESCALATION) for the five options A-E and full diagnostic. Not carving another subticket autonomously — this is a design decision, not a one-line bug fix.

Current epic state:
- 40.1 (S1) closed at 5a958ce
- 40.3 (S3) closed at 677a209
- 40.4 (S1b) closed at ebcd4e1 — scope-expansion subticket
- 40.2 (S2) open, blocked pending direction. Test rebased to e8c04aa, still red on telemetry.app missing.

ORCHESTRATED
Children closed: 4 (S1, S2, S3 + S1b scope expansion)
BOUNCEs encountered: 1 (S2 round 1 — mix-in-mix lock deadlock; respawn redesigned with Mix.Task.run in-process)
Scope expansions: 1 (S1b for :code.add_pathz charlist cast)
User escalations: 1 (telemetry transitive-dep gap; user overrode with "merge and clean up")

Epic branch think-pvr/archive-install-fix at e8c04aa. make smoke 640/0 (19 excluded). Default lane unaffected.

Known limitation persisting at close: mix archive.build does not bundle mutagen_ex's telemetry runtime dep, so `mix archive.install ./mutagen_ex-X.Y.Z.ez && mix mutagen` against a host with no telemetry dep still aborts with runtime_load_failed (rather than the original MatchError). The v11 stub command `mix test test/integration/archive_install_test.exs --include archive_integration` is red on that scenario. S2's test correctly documents the failure mode; resolution is deferred per user direction (see options A-E on mutagen-wrd.40.2's ORCHESTRATOR_ESCALATION comment).

title: Archive-install adoption
